# assertion "Function already has body?" in CodeGenFunction::StartFunction when emitting duplicate extern inline definition of memcpy

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173608
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** clang:codegen, crash, generated by fuzzer

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/74cb3Ts73

- clang start crashing on `Trunk`
- generated by fuzzer

## Code
```c
typedef unsigned long size_t;

#define AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY                                                   \
  extern inline __attribute__((always_inline)) __attribute__((gnu_inline))

#define AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY_ALTERNATE                                         \
  extern inline __attribute__((__always_inline__))                             \
  __attribute__((__artificial__))


AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY void *memcpy(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memcpy.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memcpy(a, b, c);
}

AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY_ALTERNATE void *memmove(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memmove.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memmove(a, b, c);
}

void *foo(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) { return memcpy(a, b, c); }

AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY void *memcpy(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memcpy.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memcpy(a, b, c);
}
```

## Code
```c
typedef unsigned long size_t;

#define AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY                                                   \
  extern inline __attribute__((always_inline)) __attribute__((gnu_inline))

#define AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY_ALTERNATE                                         \
  extern inline __attribute__((__always_inline__))                             \
  __attribute__((__artificial__))


AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY void *memcpy(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memcpy.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memcpy(a, b, c);
}

AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY_ALTERNATE void *memmove(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memmove.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memmove(a, b, c);
}

void *foo(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) { return memcpy(a, b, c); }

AVAILABLE_EXTERNALLY void *memcpy(void *a, const void *b, size_t c) {
  asm("# memcpy.inline marker");
  return __builtin_memcpy(a, b, c);
}
```
</details>


---

### Comment 2 - keinflue

Assertion since Clang 14: https://godbolt.org/z/ajqPsada6

Further reduction (https://godbolt.org/z/drY3Pdnsj):
```cpp
typedef unsigned long size_t;

extern inline __attribute__((always_inline)) __attribute__((gnu_inline)) void*
memcpy(void* a, const void* b, size_t c) {}

void* foo(void* a, const void* b, size_t c) { return memcpy(a, b, c); }

inline void* memcpy(void* a, const void* b, size_t c) {}
```

Occurs only in C mode, not C++ mode, and seems to be specific to the `memcpy` signature. Changing the function name or e.g. type used for `size_t` does not result in assertion. Attributes and specifiers are required to reproduce the issue.

Possible duplicate affecting `memcmp` instead of `memcpy`: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/110774

---
